item.title: The foods you should avoid as the cyclosporiasis outbreak grows
item.description: Over 1,600 cases of cyclosporiasis have now been confirmed as the CDC continues its search for the origin of the outbreak. CBS News' Megan Cerullo reports on the precautions consumers should take.

item.title: Here's how daylight saving time was created as House passes bill to make it permanent
item.description: Lindsey Reiser takes a look at the history of daylight saving time.

item.title: U.S., Iran exchange strikes for fifth straight day, Trump says, "they better behave"
item.description: The U.S. launched back-to-back strikes on Iran Wednesday, marking a fifth straight day of attacks. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: Wildfires expose millions in Midwest, Northeast to dangerous smoke
item.description: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing large swaths of the Midwest and Northeast U.S. this week.

item.title: Trump to allege Chinese meddling in U.S. elections in primetime speech, sources say
item.description: Part of President Trump's speech Thursday night is expected to touch on previously unreported alleged Chinese meddling in U.S. elections, according to sources familiar with the matter.

item.title: Pentagon proposes new secrecy power to withhold unclassified records from FOIA
item.description: The legislative proposal would allow the defense secretary to withhold "controlled unclassified information," potentially curbing public access to a wide range of defense records.

item.title: Florida DMV hands 76-year-old a license plate that became the talk of her senior community
item.description: Some people ask and pay for funny license plates, but this Florida woman received plates at random that make people laugh. Tony Dokoupil has the story.

item.title: United Airlines anticipates $6 billion in added fuel costs
item.description: U.S. airlines say higher jet fuel prices are costing them billions more than they anticipated at the beginning of the year.

item.title: Blanche faces questions on DOJ fund, Epstein files at Senate confirmation hearing
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche testified Wednesday before the Senate Judiciary Committee for his confirmation hearing to lead the Justice Department in a permanent capacity.

item.title: DHS says all ICE arrest teams will have agent with body camera, Trump reinstates ICE traffic stops
item.description: After federal immigration agents shot and killed two people this month, the Department of Homeland Security now says every arrest team going forward will have at least one officer wearing a body camera. Also on Wednesday, President Trump overturned an order that would've stopped ICE agents from performing some traffic stops. CBS News homeland security correspondent Nicole Sganga has more details on ICE policy changes.

item.title: Iowa AG Brenna Bird on her support for Todd Blanche as attorney general
item.description: Iowa Attorney General Brenna Bird joins "The Takeout" to discuss Todd Blanche's record as a prosecutor and make her case for why he should be confirmed as attorney general while the Senate weighs his candidacy.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Senators on both sides question Todd Blanche's independence
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, President Trump's former personal lawyer, faced questions at his confirmation hearing on Wednesday. He tried to reassure key Republicans, but not everyone was convinced. Nikole Killion has more.

item.title: Argentina rallies past bitter rival England to book spot in World Cup final
item.description: Defending World Cup champion Argentina rallied to edge England 2-1 to advance to Sunday's final at MetLife Stadium, where they will face Spain.

item.title: Todd Blanche and Jay Clayton grilled by Democrats in separate confirmation hearings
item.description: Todd Blanche and Jay Clayton faced tough questions Wednesday in their respective Senate confirmation hearings for attorney general and director of national intelligence. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has more details.

item.title: Nolan Wells' family meets with Mississippi district attorney over his death
item.description: The parents of Nolan Wells and their legal team received assurances from the Mississippi district attorney on Wednesday that the investigation into the teenager's death would be thorough. Skyler Henry has more details on the case.

item.title: Boat capsizes in San Francisco Bay, killing 1, 3 still missing
item.description: After a three-story cabin cruiser capsized in the San Francisco Bay on Tuesday, a 79-year-old man died, and three people were still missing 24 hours later. Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

item.title: U.S. disables ship for allegedly violating Iran blockade amid Strait of Hormuz feud
item.description: For the first time since President Trump reinstated a blockade on Iranian ports, the U.S. military has disabled a ship that allegedly tried to sail to Kharg Island.

item.title: Canada wildfires darken U.S. skies, trigger air quality alerts
item.description: Hundreds of wildfires in Canada darkened skies in the U.S. on Wednesday. A big shift in the wind prompted air quality alerts in at least 16 states across the Midwest and Northeast. Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: Argentina beats England to reach FIFA World Cup final
item.description: Major League Soccer analyst Kacey White joins "The Takeout" to break down Argentina's last-minute semifinal win against England and what to expect from the World Cup final.

item.title: 103 House Democrats vote to cut off aid to Israel
item.description: More than 100 House Democrats vote to cut off aid to Israel on Wednesday, the latest flashpoint over support for the U.S. ally. Kendra Barkoff Lamy, former press secretary to then-Vice President Joe Biden, and Marc Short, former Chief of Staff for Vice President Mike Pence, join "The Takeout" to discuss

item.title: Maine congressional candidate Matt Dunlap on his campaign, Graham Platner
item.description: Maine Democrat Matt Dunlap joins "The Takeout" to discuss his race for U.S. House and address Graham Platner's controversial Senate campaign.

item.title: GPS data tracks boat Nolan Wells was on before he went missing
item.description: GPS data from the Mississippi Department of Marine Resources, obtained by CBS News, tracks the movements of the boat that carried Nolan Wells to Horn Island on July 4.

item.title: Argentina stuns England in World Cup semifinal with late comeback
item.description: One of the World Cup's great rivalries ended with one of the World Cup's best comebacks. Argentina scored two goals in seven minutes, stunning England 2-1 in Wednesday's semifinal. Lionel Messi had two assists, including on the winning goal in the final minutes. Nicole Valdes reports.

item.title: England-Argentina World Cup semifinal reignites painful historic rivalry
item.description: The World Cup semifinal between England and Argentina was a grudge match, reopening decades-old wounds for both countries.

item.title: Hegseth requires testosterone deficiency screening for service members over 30
item.description: The Pentagon will now require service members over 30 to screen for testosterone deficiency, and if needed, they can elect to have testosterone replacement therapy.

item.title: Senior defense officials looking at Cuba military options
item.description: Military planners have examined options for possible action against Cuba, including an Army-led air assault involving the 101st Airborne Division.

item.title: Here's what the Common Cents Act means for businesses, consumers
item.description: New law aims to address how businesses and consumers should transact amid the phase-out of the penny.

item.title: Hilton employee who sent racial slur to WNBA star is fired
item.description: Hilton Grand Vacations said it has fired an employee who sent a racial slur to Las Vegas Aces guard Chelsea Gray.

item.title: Alzheimer's blood test may show who's likely to develop it in 5 to 10 years
item.description: A blood test may predict if apparently healthy older adults are likely to develop Alzheimer's symptoms in the next five or 10 years, researchers say.
